title: strychnos cogens
intro: Strychnos cogens, also known as Strychnine tree or Nux Vomica, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, whose seeds contain the highly toxic alkaloid strychnine. Traditionally, the seeds of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making them a treatment for various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In addition to its medicinal properties, the plant has been used for its health benefits, including reducing muscle spasms, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic. Strychnos cogens is often prepared as a tincture, powder, or decoction, and is used in traditional medicine systems such as Ayurveda and Unani.
